events.text: Patient is a 58-year-old Caucasian female, height 5 feet 4 inches, weight 145 pounds, ECOG performance status 1.

At age 58, patient presented with progressive fatigue and recurrent upper respiratory infections over 6 months; workup revealed hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, calcium 10.8 mg/dL, beta-2 microglobulin 4.2 mg/L, albumin 3.6 g/dL consistent with ISS Stage II multiple myeloma.

At age 58, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 45% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138 and cytoplasmic IgA lambda; CD56 weakly positive; Ki-67 proliferation index 15%.

At age 58, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate revealed t(4;14) in 68% of plasma cells and gain(1q21) in 52% of cells; no del(17p) or t(14;16) detected; classified as high-risk cytogenetics; R-ISS Stage II based on ISS II, LDH normal at 190 U/L, but high-risk FISH present.

At age 58, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 3.1 g/dL in the beta region; immunofixation confirmed IgA lambda monoclonal protein; serum free light chain lambda 245 mg/L, kappa 18 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 0.07; quantitative immunoglobulins showed IgA 3800 mg/dL with suppressed IgG 420 mg/dL and IgM 28 mg/dL.

At age 58, PET/CT showed FDG-avid lytic lesions in the thoracic spine at T5 and T9, left scapula, right iliac bone, and left proximal femur; maximum SUV 8.4 in the T5 vertebral body lesion; no extramedullary plasmacytomas.

At age 58, MRI of the whole spine demonstrated diffuse patchy marrow signal abnormality consistent with myeloma infiltration; compression deformity at T5 with 40% height loss but no cord compression; additional focal lesions at T9 and L3.

At age 58, patient initiated VRd induction with bortezomib 1.3 mg/m2 subcutaneously weekly on days 1, 8, and 15, lenalidomide 25 mg days 1 through 21, and dexamethasone 40 mg weekly on a 28-day cycle; given high-risk cytogenetics, plan for 6 cycles of induction before transplant consideration.
At age 58, cycle 1 of VRd administered; patient tolerated treatment well with grade 1 insomnia and mild ankle edema attributed to dexamethasone.
At age 58, cycle 2 of VRd completed; repeat labs showed M-spike decreased to 2.0 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain decreased to 120 mg/L; hemoglobin stable at 10.5 g/dL.

At age 58, after cycle 3 of VRd, patient developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelet count 62,000; lenalidomide dose reduced to 15 mg; also noted grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with intermittent fingertip tingling.

At age 58, cycles 4 through 6 of VRd completed with dose modifications; post-induction SPEP showed M-spike 0.3 g/dL; serum free lambda light chain 28 mg/L; assessment classified as very good partial response per IMWG criteria.

At age 58, pre-transplant evaluation showed adequate organ function; cardiac ejection fraction 60% on echocardiogram; pulmonary function tests within normal limits; patient proceeded to stem cell collection.

At age 59, patient received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 conditioning followed by autologous stem cell transplant; stem cell dose infused was 4.8 million CD34-positive cells per kilogram; engraftment achieved on day plus 14.

At age 59, post-transplant course complicated by grade 2 mucositis and grade 3 diarrhea requiring IV hydration; febrile neutropenia on day plus 7 treated with piperacillin-tazobactam; blood cultures negative; symptoms resolved by day plus 18.

At age 59, day plus 100 bone marrow biopsy showed 2% plasma cells with normal phenotype on flow cytometry; SPEP showed no M-spike; immunofixation showed faint IgA lambda band; MRD by multiparameter flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity was positive with 0.02% residual aberrant plasma cells.

At age 59, day plus 100 response assessment classified as complete response per IMWG but MRD positive; given high-risk cytogenetics with t(4;14), decision made to start lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg daily with plan for extended maintenance duration.
At age 59, lenalidomide maintenance started at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; tolerated well with grade 1 fatigue; monthly CBC monitoring initiated.
At age 60, annual PET/CT showed resolution of previously FDG-avid lesions; sclerotic healing changes in previously lytic sites; no new disease activity.

At age 60, one-year post-transplant labs showed SPEP no M-spike, immunofixation negative achieving stringent complete response; repeat MRD by flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity now negative; lenalidomide maintenance continued.

At age 61, patient developed left lower extremity deep vein thrombosis while on lenalidomide maintenance; diagnosed by duplex ultrasound showing occlusive thrombus in the left popliteal vein; started on therapeutic enoxaparin then transitioned to apixaban; lenalidomide continued with close monitoring.

At age 62, continued in stringent complete response on lenalidomide maintenance; anticoagulation with apixaban continued; peripheral neuropathy resolved; ECOG performance status 0; patient tolerating 10 mg lenalidomide without dose modification.

At age 63, after 4 years of maintenance, routine monitoring showed sustained stringent complete response; discussed potential discontinuation of maintenance given MRD negativity but elected to continue given high-risk cytogenetics per physician recommendation.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 58, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 45% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138 and cytoplasmic IgA lambda; CD56 weakly positive; Ki-67 proliferation index 15%.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 58, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate revealed t(4;14) in 68% of plasma cells and gain(1q21) in 52% of cells; no del(17p) or t(14;16) detected; classified as high-risk cytogenetics; R-ISS Stage II based on ISS II, LDH normal at 190 U/L, but high-risk FISH present.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

At age 58, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 3.1 g/dL in the beta region; immunofixation confirmed IgA lambda monoclonal protein; serum free light chain lambda 245 mg/L, kappa 18 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 0.07; quantitative immunoglobulins showed IgA 3800 mg/dL with suppressed IgG 420 mg/dL and IgM 28 mg/dL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 58, PET/CT showed FDG-avid lytic lesions in the thoracic spine at T5 and T9, left scapula, right iliac bone, and left proximal femur; maximum SUV 8.4 in the T5 vertebral body lesion; no extramedullary plasmacytomas.

Please correlate clinically.

Please correlate clinically.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 60, annual PET/CT showed resolution of previously FDG-avid lesions; sclerotic healing changes in previously lytic sites; no new disease activity.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 62, continued in stringent complete response on lenalidomide maintenance; anticoagulation with apixaban continued; peripheral neuropathy resolved; ECOG performance status 0; patient tolerating 10 mg lenalidomide without dose modification.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Multiple myeloma, ISS stage II-III. IgG kappa or IgA lambda subtypes. Treated with VRd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction followed by autologous stem cell transplant and lenalidomide maintenance. Include serum protein electrophoresis, free light chains, beta-2 microglobulin, FISH for del(17p) and t(4;14).
